pull_request.title: feat(pygal): implement histogram-capability
pull_request.body:
## Implementation: `histogram-capability` - pygal

Implements the **pygal** version of `histogram-capability`.

**File:** `plots/histogram-capability/implementations/pygal.py`

**Parent Issue:** #4552

---
:robot: *[impl-generate workflow](https://github.com/MarkusNeusinger/pyplots/actions/runs/23318228545)*

changed_files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+""" pyplots.ai
+histogram-capability: Process Capability Plot with Specification Limits
+Library: pygal 3.1.0 | Python 3.14.3
+Quality: 82/100 | Created: 2026-03-19
+"""
+
+import numpy as np
+import pygal
+from pygal.style import Style
+from scipy import stats
+
+
+# Data - Shaft diameter measurements (mm)
+np.random.seed(42)
+lsl = 9.95
+usl = 10.05
+target = 10.00
+measurements = np.random.normal(loc=10.002, scale=0.012, size=200)
+
+# Statistics
+mean = np.mean(measurements)
+sigma = np.std(measurements, ddof=1)
+cp = (usl - lsl) / (6 * sigma)
+cpk = min((usl - mean) / (3 * sigma), (mean - lsl) / (3 * sigma))
+
+# Histogram bins
+n_bins = 20
+counts, bin_edges = np.histogram(measurements, bins=n_bins)
+bin_width = bin_edges[1] - bin_edges[0]
+
+# Normal curve - use fewer, wider bars for a clean envelope look
+n_curve_pts = 60
+x_curve = np.linspace(mean - 4 * sigma, mean + 4 * sigma, n_curve_pts)
+y_curve = stats.norm.pdf(x_curve, mean, sigma) * len(measurements) * bin_width
+dx_curve = x_curve[1] - x_curve[0]
+
+# Style - colorblind-safe palette with distinct colors per series
+# Order: histogram(blue), curve(purple), LSL(orange), USL(orange), target(teal), mean(gray)
+custom_style = Style(
+    background="white",
+    plot_background="white",
+    foreground="#333333",
+    foreground_strong="#333333",
+    foreground_subtle="#e0e0e0",
+    colors=("#306998", "#8e44ad", "#d35400", "#d35400", "#16a085", "#555555"),
+    title_font_size=64,
+    label_font_size=44,
+    major_label_font_size=40,
+    legend_font_size=38,
+    value_font_size=28,
+    stroke_width=4,
+    opacity=0.80,
+    opacity_hover=0.90,
+    font_family="'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif",
+)
+
+# Chart
+y_ceil = float(max(counts) * 1.3)
+chart = pygal.Histogram(
+    width=4800,
+    height=2700,
+    style=custom_style,
+    title="histogram-capability · pygal · pyplots.ai  |  Cp = {:.2f}  ·  Cpk = {:.2f}".format(cp, cpk),
+    x_title="Shaft Diameter (mm)",
+    y_title="Frequency",
+    show_legend=True,
+    legend_at_bottom=True,
+    legend_box_size=28,
+    show_y_guides=True,
+    show_x_guides=False,
+    truncate_label=-1,
+    truncate_legend=-1,
+    margin_top=40,
+    margin_right=60,
+    margin_bottom=30,
+    margin_left=20,
+    x_value_formatter=lambda x: f"{x:.3f}",
+    y_value_formatter=lambda y: f"{y:.0f}",
+    xrange=(lsl - 3 * sigma, usl + 3 * sigma),
+    range=(0, y_ceil),
+    css=[
+        "file://style.css",
+        "inline:.plot .background {fill: white !important; stroke: none !important;}",
+        "inline:.graph > .background {fill: white !important; stroke: none !important;}",
+        "inline:.axis .guides .line {stroke: #e0e0e0 !important; stroke-width: 0.8px;}",
+        "inline:.axis.x > path.line {stroke: none !important;}",
+        "inline:.axis.y > path.line {stroke: none !important;}",
+        "inline:text.title {font-weight: 600 !important; fill: #222222 !important;}",
+        "inline:.legends text {font-weight: 400 !important; fill: #444444 !important;}",
+    ],
+    js=[],
+)
+
+# Histogram bars using pygal.Histogram native format: (height, start, end)
+hist_data = [(float(counts[i]), float(bin_edges[i]), float(bin_edges[i + 1])) for i in range(len(counts))]
+chart.add("Measurements", hist_data)
+
+# Normal distribution curve - fewer wider bars for smooth envelope appearance
+curve_data = [(float(y), float(x), float(x + dx_curve)) for x, y in zip(x_curve, y_curve, strict=True)]
+chart.add("Normal fit", curve_data, stroke_style={"width": 3, "linecap": "round"})
+
+# LSL vertical line - wide bar for visibility
+line_width = 0.0008
+chart.add(
+    "LSL (9.95)",
+    [(y_ceil, float(lsl - line_width), float(lsl + line_width))],
+    stroke_style={"width": 8, "dasharray": "18,8"},
+)
+
+# USL vertical line
+chart.add(
+    "USL (10.05)",
+    [(y_ceil, float(usl - line_width), float(usl + line_width))],
+    stroke_style={"width": 8, "dasharray": "18,8"},
+)
+
+# Target vertical line - teal, distinct from orange spec limits
+chart.add(
+    "Target (10.00)",
+    [(y_ceil, float(target - line_width), float(target + line_width))],
+    stroke_style={"width": 6, "dasharray": "12,6"},
+)
+
+# Mean vertical line - dark gray, dotted pattern
+chart.add(
+    f"Mean ({mean:.3f})",
+    [(y_ceil, float(mean - line_width / 2), float(mean + line_width / 2))],
+    stroke_style={"width": 4, "dasharray": "4,8"},
+)
+
+# Save
+chart.render_to_png("plot.png")
+chart.render_to_file("plot.html")

@@ -0,0 +1,238 @@

+created: '2026-03-19T21:44:26Z'
+updated: '2026-03-19T22:20:36Z'

+issue: 4552

+preview_url: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/histogram-capability/pygal/plot.png
+preview_thumb: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/histogram-capability/pygal/plot_thumb.png
+preview_html: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/histogram-capability/pygal/plot.html
+quality_score: 82
+review:
+  strengths:
+  - 'Perfect code quality: clean KISS structure, reproducible seed, elegant workarounds
+    for pygal limitations'
+  - Strong data quality with realistic manufacturing context (shaft diameter measurements)
+  - 'Good use of pygal-specific features: CSS overrides for spine removal, inline
+    styling, native Histogram format'
+  - 'Comprehensive feature set: histogram, normal fit, LSL/USL, target, mean, Cp/Cpk
+    all present'
+  - Custom style with explicit font sizes, custom font family, and intentional color
+    palette
+  weaknesses:
+  - LSL/USL specification limit lines render as tall pink bars at plot edges, looking
+    like decorative borders rather than meaningful reference lines
+  - Normal distribution curve appears as stepped bars rather than a smooth curve (pygal
+    limitation)
+  - Mean line (gray) is hard to distinguish from target line (teal) at this scale
+  - Title is right-aligned rather than centered, creating slight visual imbalance
+  image_description: The plot displays a process capability histogram rendered with
+    pygal. Blue histogram bars represent 200 shaft diameter measurements (mm) distributed
+    in a bell-curve shape centered near 10.002. A lavender/purple normal distribution
+    fit is overlaid as many thin histogram bars creating a bell-curve envelope shape.
+    Two tall pink/salmon vertical bars at the extreme left and right edges of the
+    plot mark LSL (9.95) and USL (10.05) — these visually resemble border decorations
+    rather than distinct specification limit lines. A thin teal vertical line marks
+    the Target (10.00) at center, and a thin gray vertical line marks the Mean (10.002).
+    The title at top right reads "histogram-capability · pygal · pyplots.ai | Cp =
+    1.49 · Cpk = 1.45". X-axis is labeled "Shaft Diameter (mm)" ranging from 9.950
+    to 10.050. Y-axis is labeled "Frequency" ranging from 0 to 40. A six-entry legend
+    at the bottom identifies Measurements, Normal fit, LSL (9.95), USL (10.05), Target
+    (10.00), and Mean (10.002). Background is clean white with subtle horizontal grid
+    lines and axis spines removed via CSS.
+  criteria_checklist:
+    visual_quality:
+      score: 25
+      max: 30
+      items:
+      - id: VQ-01
+        name: Text Legibility
+        score: 7
+        max: 8
+        passed: true
+        comment: 'All font sizes explicitly set (title=64, label=44, major_label=40,
+          legend=38, value=28). All text clearly readable. Minor: title right-aligned.'
+      - id: VQ-02
+        name: No Overlap
+        score: 6
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: No text collisions. Legend entries well-spaced. X-axis labels clear.
+      - id: VQ-03
+        name: Element Visibility
+        score: 4
+        max: 6
+        passed: false
+        comment: LSL/USL rendered as tall wide pink bars at extreme edges — read as
+          border decorations. Mean line hard to distinguish.
+      - id: VQ-04
+        name: Color Accessibility
+        score: 3
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: Blue, purple, pink, teal, gray mostly distinguishable. Purple/blue
+          overlap could challenge some colorblind types.
+      - id: VQ-05
+        name: Layout & Canvas
+        score: 3
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: Plot fills good portion of canvas. Title right-alignment creates
+          slight imbalance.
+      - id: VQ-06
+        name: Axis Labels & Title
+        score: 2
+        max: 2
+        passed: true
+        comment: Shaft Diameter (mm) with units, Frequency — descriptive and appropriate.
+    design_excellence:
+      score: 12
+      max: 20
+      items:
+      - id: DE-01
+        name: Aesthetic Sophistication
+        score: 5
+        max: 8
+        passed: true
+        comment: Custom color palette, custom font family, CSS overrides for spine
+          removal. Goes beyond defaults. Pink LSL/USL bars reduce polish.
+      - id: DE-02
+        name: Visual Refinement
+        score: 4
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: CSS removes axis spines and axis lines, y-guides only, white background,
+          custom fonts.
+      - id: DE-03
+        name: Data Storytelling
+        score: 3
+        max: 6
+        passed: false
+        comment: Cp/Cpk in title tells capability story. LSL/USL rendering as border-like
+          bars diminishes narrative clarity.
+    spec_compliance:
+      score: 14
+      max: 15
+      items:
+      - id: SC-01
+        name: Plot Type
+        score: 5
+        max: 5
+        passed: true
+        comment: Histogram with normal distribution overlay — correct chart type.
+      - id: SC-02
+        name: Required Features
+        score: 3
+        max: 4

+        comment: All features present. Normal curve is stepped bars (pygal limitation).

+      - id: SC-03
+        name: Data Mapping
+        score: 3
+        max: 3
+        passed: true
+        comment: X-axis correctly maps shaft diameter, Y-axis maps frequency.
+      - id: SC-04
+        name: Title & Legend
+        score: 3

+        comment: Title format correct with Cp/Cpk. Legend labels descriptive with
+          actual values.
+    data_quality:

+      items:
+      - id: DQ-01

+      - id: DQ-02

+          machining.
+    code_quality:

+      - id: CQ-02

+      - id: CQ-03
